title: Invalid month option can generate corrupt period data
reasoning: The signature says --month must be 1-12, but handle() casts arbitrary input and passes it to Carbon. Carbon can normalize overflow values such as 13 into a different calendar month while the raw invalid month is still persisted to contributions.month. That can create rows like year=2026, month=13 with a due_date in January 2027, breaking period identity and later queries that assume months are 1-12.
reproduction: Run php artisan contributions:generate --year=2026 --month=13 for a family with an active categorized member, then inspect the generated Contribution row.
recommendation: Validate month and year before any date construction or database writes. Reject month values outside 1-12 with a clear error and non-success exit code.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No linked tests were provided, and there is no included test asserting invalid command options are rejected.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a Pest console test that runs contributions:generate with --month=13 and asserts a failure exit code and zero created Contribution rows.
minimumFixScope: Add option validation in GenerateMonthlyContributions::handle and cover invalid date options with a focused console test.
